1.arabic: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ تَنزِيلُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْحَكِيمِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: THE REVELATION OF this Book is from God, the mighty and all-wise.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The revelation of the Book is from Allah, the Most Honourable, the Wise.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: The sending down of the Book is from God the All-mighty, the All-wise.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: The revelation of this Book is from Allah, the Mighty, the Wise.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The revelation of this Book (the Quran) is from Allah, the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The revelation of the Book is from God, the Mighty and Wise.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: The revelation of this Book is from Allah, the Most Mighty, the Most Wise.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The revelation of this Book is from Allah, the Almighty, the All-Wise.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: The revelation of the Scripture is from Allah, the Mighty, the Wise.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The [gradual] sending down of the Book is from Allah, the All-mighty, the All-wise.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: The sending down of the Book is from Allah, the Almighty, the Wise,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The revelation of the Qur'an is from Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Wise.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: This Book is a revelation from God, the Majestic and All-Wise.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The revelation of the Book is from Allah, the Mighty, the Wise.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: This Book is sent down by God the Mighty, the Wise.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The revelation of this Book is from Allah, the Exalted in Power, full of Wisdom.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Tanzeelu alkitabi mina Allahi alAAazeezi alhakeemi

2.arabic: إِنَّآ أَنزَلْنَآ إِلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِٱلْحَقِّ فَٱعْبُدِ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِصًا لَّهُ ٱلدِّينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We have revealed to you the Scripture with exactitude; so worship God with devotion all exclusive for Him.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: We have indeed divinely revealed the Book to you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) with the truth, therefore worship Allah, as His sincere bondman.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: We have sent down to thee the Book with the truth; so worship God, making thy religion His sincerely.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily We! We have sent down the Book Unto thee With truth: wherefore worship thou Allah, making exclusion for Him in religion.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily, We have sent down the Book to you (O Muhammad SAW) in truth: So worship Allah (Alone) by doing religious deeds sincerely for Allah's sake only, (and not to show-off, and not to set up rivals with Him in worship).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We sent down to you the Book with the truth, so serve God, devoting your religion to Him.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (O Prophet), it is We Who have revealed this Book to you with Truth. So serve only Allah, consecrating your devotion to Him.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, We have sent down the Book to you in truth. So, worship Allah by doing religious deeds sincerely for Him only.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! We have revealed the Scripture unto thee (Muhammad) with truth; so worship Allah, making religion pure for Him (only).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed We have sent down the Book to you with the truth; so worship Allah, putting exclusive faith in Him.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We have sent down the Book with the truth to you (Prophet Muhammad), therefore worship Allah, and make your religion sincerely His.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, We have sent down to you the Book, [O Muhammad], in truth. So worship Allah, [being] sincere to Him in religion.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We have revealed the Book to you in all truth. Worship God and be devoted to His religion.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely We have revealed to you the Book with the truth, therefore serve Allah, being sincere to Him in obedience.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: It is We who sent down the Book to you [Prophet] with the Truth, so worship God with your total devotion:

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Verily it is We Who have revealed the Book to thee in Truth: so serve Allah, offering Him sincere devotion.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna anzalna ilayka alkitaba bialhaqqi faoAAbudi Allaha mukhlisan lahu alddeena

3.arabic: أَلَا لِلَّهِ ٱلدِّينُ ٱلْخَالِصُ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ أَوْلِيَآءَ مَا نَعْبُدُهُمْ إِلَّا لِيُقَرِّبُونَآ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ زُلْفَىٰٓ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَهُمْ فِى مَا هُمْ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِى مَنْ هُوَ كَـٰذِبٌ كَفَّارٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Remember that devotion is exclusively for God. Those who have taken protectors other than Him, say: "We worship them that they may bring us nearer to God." Surely God will judge between them in what they are differing about. Verily God does not show the way to an ungrateful liar.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Pay heed! Worship is for Allah only; and those who have taken others as their supporters beside Him say; “We worship* them only so that they get us closer to Allah”; Allah will surely judge between them regarding the matter in which they dispute; indeed Allah does not guide one who is a big liar, extremely ungrateful. (*The pagans regarded their idols as smaller Gods and worshipped them. So did the Christians. This does not apply to Muslims who only respect their elders, and ask for their blessings.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Belongs not sincere religion to God? And those who take protectors, apart from Him -- 'We only serve them that they may bring us nigh in nearness to God' -- surely God shall judge between them touching that whereon they are at variance. Surely God guides not him who is a liar, unthankful.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: LO! for Allah is the religion exclusive. And those who take patrons beside Him, saying: We Worship them not save in order that they may bring us nigh Unto God in approach - -verily Allah will judge between them concerning that wherein they differ. Verily Allah guideth not him who is a liar and ingrate.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Surely, the religion (i.e. the worship and the obedience) is for Allah only. And those who take Auliya' (protectors and helpers) besides Him (say): "We worship them only that they may bring us near to Allah." Verily, Allah will judge between them concerning that wherein they differ. Truly, Allah guides not him who is a liar, and a disbeliever.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Is not to God that sincere faith is due? As for those who take guardians besides Him, “We only worship them that they may bring us nearer to God.” God will judge between them regarding their differences. God does not guide the lying blasphemer.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Lo, religion is exclusively devoted to Allah. Your religion is entirely consecrated to Him. As for those who have taken others than Allah for their guardians, (they say): “We worship them only that they may bring us nearer to Allah.” Allah will judge between them concerning what they differ about. Verily Allah does not guide anyone who is given to sheer lying, is an utter unbeliever.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Surely, the religion is for Allah only. And those who take protectors besides Him (say): "We worship them only that they may bring us near to Allah." Verily, Allah will judge between them concerning that wherein they differ. Truly, Allah guides not him who is a liar, and a disbeliever.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Surely pure religion is for Allah only. And those who choose protecting friends beside Him (say): We worship them only that they may bring us near unto Allah. Lo! Allah will judge between them concerning that wherein they differ. Lo! Allah guideth not him who is a liar, an ingrate.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed, only exclusive faith is worthy of Allah, and those who take other as awliya besides Him [claiming,] ‘We only worship them so that they may bring us near to Allah,’ Allah will judge between them concerning that about which they differ. Indeed Allah does not guide someone who is a liar and an ingrate.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Is it not that the sincere religion belongs to Allah? As for those who choose guardians, other than Him, (saying): 'We only worship them so that they will bring us near to Allah' Indeed, Allah will judge between them concerning their differences. Indeed, Allah does not guide he who is an unthankful liar.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Unquestionably, for Allah is the pure religion. And those who take protectors besides Him [say], "We only worship them that they may bring us nearer to Allah in position." Indeed, Allah will judge between them concerning that over which they differ. Indeed, Allah does not guide he who is a liar and [confirmed] disbeliever.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: The religion of God is certainly pure. Concerning those whom they consider as their guardians besides God, they say, "We only worship them so that they may make our positions nearer to God." God will certainly issue His decree about their differences. God does not guide the liars and the disbelievers.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Now, surely, sincere obedience is due to Allah (alone) and (as for) those who take guardians besides Him, (saying), We do not serve them save that they may make us nearer to Allah, surely Allah will judge between them in that in which they differ; surely Allah does not guide him aright who is a liar, ungrateful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: it is to God alone that sincere obedience is due. And those who take other guardians besides Him say, "We serve them only that they may bring us nearer to God." Surely, God will judge between them concerning that wherein they differ. God does not guide anyone who is bent on lying and is a disbelieving liar.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Is it not to Allah that sincere devotion is due? But those who take for protectors other than Allah (say): "We only serve them in order that they may bring us nearer to Allah." Truly Allah will judge between them in that wherein they differ. But Allah guides not such as are false and ungrateful.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ala lillahi alddeenu alkhalisu waallatheena ittakhathoo min doonihi awliyaa ma naAAbuduhum illa liyuqarriboona ila Allahi zulfa inna Allaha yahkumu baynahum fee ma hum feehi yakhtalifoona inna Allaha la yahdee man huwa kathibun kaffarun

4.arabic: لَّوْ أَرَادَ ٱللَّهُ أَن يَتَّخِذَ وَلَدًا لَّٱصْطَفَىٰ مِمَّا يَخْلُقُ مَا يَشَآءُ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ ۖ هُوَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْوَٰحِدُ ٱلْقَهَّارُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Had God pleased to take a son, He could have chosen whom He liked from among those He has created. Glory be to Him. He is God, the one, the omnipotent.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Were Allah to create a son* for Himself, He would have chosen any one from His creation! Purity is to Him! He is Allah, the One, the All Dominant. (*Which is impossible.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Had God desired to take to Him a son, He would have chosen whatever He willed of that He has created. Glory be to Him! He is God, the One, the Omnipotent.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Had Allah willed to take a son, He could have chosen whatsoever He pleased out of that which He hath created. Hallowed be He! He is Allah the One, the Subduer.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Had Allah willed to take a son (or offspring or children), He could have chosen whom He pleased out of those whom He created. But glory be to Him! (He is above such things). He is Allah, the One, the Irresistible.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: If God wanted to have a son, He could have selected from His creation at will. Glory be to Him. He is God, the One, the Prevailing.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: If Allah had wanted to take to Himself a son, He could have chosen anyone He wanted out of those whom He creates. Glory be to Him (that He should have a son). He is Allah: the One, the Overpowering.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Had Allah willed to take a son, He could have chosen whom He willed out of those whom He created. But glory be to Him! He is Allah, the One, the Irresistible.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: If Allah had willed to choose a son, He could have chosen what He would of that which He hath created. Be He Glorified! He is Allah, the One, the Absolute.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Had Allah intended to take a son, He could have chosen from those He has created whatever He wished. Immaculate is He! He is Allah, the One, the All-paramount.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If Allah had desired to take a son, He would have chosen whatever He willed out of His creation. Exaltations to Him! He is Allah, the One, the Conqueror.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: If Allah had intended to take a son, He could have chosen from what He creates whatever He willed. Exalted is He; He is Allah, the One, the Prevailing.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Had God wanted to have a son, He would have chosen one from His creatures according to His will. God is too Exalted to have a son. He is One and Almighty.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: If Allah desire to take a son to Himself, He will surely choose those He pleases from what He has created. Glory be to Him: He is Allah, the One, the Subduer (of all).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: If God had willed to take a son He could have chosen anyone He pleased out of His creation: but Glory be to Him! [He is above such things.] He is God, the One, the Omnipotent.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Had Allah wished to take to Himself a son, He could have chosen whom He pleased out of those whom He doth create: but Glory be to Him! (He is above such things.) He is Allah, the One, the Irresistible.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Law arada Allahu an yattakhitha waladan laistafa mimma yakhluqu ma yashao subhanahu huwa Allahu alwahidu alqahharu

5.arabic: خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۖ يُكَوِّرُ ٱلَّيْلَ عَلَى ٱلنَّهَارِ وَيُكَوِّرُ ٱلنَّهَارَ عَلَى ٱلَّيْلِ ۖ وَسَخَّرَ ٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ ۖ كُلٌّ يَجْرِى لِأَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۗ أَلَا هُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْغَفَّـٰرُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He has created the heavens and the earth with precision. He folds the day up over the night, and folds the night up over the day. He has subjugated the sun and moon, (so that) each runs its appointed course. Is He not all-mighty and forgiving?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: It is He Who created the heavens and the earth with the truth; He envelops the night over day and envelops the day over night, and He has subjected the sun and the moon; each one runs for its appointed term; pay heed! He only is the Most Honourable, the Oft Forgiving.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He created the heavens and the earth in truth, wrapping night about the day, and wrapping the day about the night; and He has subjected the sun and the moon, each of them running to a stated term. Is not He the All-mighty, the All-forgiving?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He hath created the heavens and the earth with truth. He rolleth the night around the day, and rolleth the day around the night, and He hath subjected the sun and the moon: each running on for a term appointed! Lo! He is the Mighty, the Forgiver.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He has created the heavens and the earth with truth. He makes the night to go in the day and makes the day to go in the night. And He has subjected the sun and the moon. Each running (on a fixed course) for an appointed term. Verily, He is the All-Mighty, the Oft-Forgiving.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He created the heavens and the earth with reason. He wraps the night around the day, and He wraps the day around the night. And He regulates the sun and the moon, each running along a specific course. He is indeed the Almighty, the Forgiver.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He created the heavens and the earth with Truth, and He folds up the day over the night and folds up the night over the day. He has subjected the sun and the moon, each is running its course until an appointed time. Lo, He is the Most Mighty, the Most Forgiving.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He has created the heavens and the earth with truth. He makes the night to go in the day and makes the day to go in the night. And He has subjected the sun and the moon. Each running for an appointed term. Verily, He is the Almighty, the Oft-Forgiving.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He hath created the heavens and the earth with truth. He maketh night to succeed day, and He maketh day to succeed night, and He constraineth the sun and the moon to give service, each running on for an appointed term. Is not He the Mighty, the Forgiver?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He created the heavens and the earth with consummate wisdom. He winds the night over the day, and winds the day over the night, and He has disposed the sun and the moon, each moving for a specified term. Indeed, He is the All-mighty, the All-forgiver!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He created the heavens and the earth in truth, wrapping the night about the day and wrapping the day about the night, He has subjected the sun and the mooneach to run for a stated term. Is He not the Almighty, the Forgiving?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He created the heavens and earth in truth. He wraps the night over the day and wraps the day over the night and has subjected the sun and the moon, each running [its course] for a specified term. Unquestionably, He is the Exalted in Might, the Perpetual Forgiver.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He has created the heavens and the earth for a genuine purpose. He covers the night with the day and the day with the night and has subdued the sun and the moon, each of which floats for an appointed time. God is certainly Majestic and All-Forgiving.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He has created the heavens and the earth with the truth; He makes the night cover the day and makes the day overtake the night, and He has made the sun and the moon subservient; each one runs on to an assigned term; now surely He is the Mighty, the great Forgiver.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He created the heavens and the earth for a true purpose; He causes the night to succeed the day and the day to succeed the night; He has subjected the sun and moon, so that they run their courses for an appointed time; He is truly the Mighty, the Forgiving.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He created the heavens and the earth in true (proportions): He makes the Night overlap the Day, and the Day overlap the Night: He has subjected the sun and the moon (to His law): Each one follows a course for a time appointed. Is not He the Exalted in Power - He Who forgives again and again?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Khalaqa alssamawati waalarda bialhaqqi yukawwiru allayla AAala alnnahari wayukawwiru alnnahara AAala allayli wasakhkhara alshshamsa waalqamara kullun yajree liajalin musamman ala huwa alAAazeezu alghaffaru

6.arabic: خَلَقَكُم مِّن نَّفْسٍ وَٰحِدَةٍ ثُمَّ جَعَلَ مِنْهَا زَوْجَهَا وَأَنزَلَ لَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ ثَمَـٰنِيَةَ أَزْوَٰجٍ ۚ يَخْلُقُكُمْ فِى بُطُونِ أُمَّهَـٰتِكُمْ خَلْقًا مِّنۢ بَعْدِ خَلْقٍ فِى ظُلُمَـٰتٍ ثَلَـٰثٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ لَهُ ٱلْمُلْكُ ۖ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ تُصْرَفُونَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He created you from a single cell, then from it created its mate; arid provided eight varieties of cattle for you. He formed you in the mother's womb, formation after formation in three (veils of) darkness. He is God your Lord. His is the kingdom. There is no god other than He. How then can you turn away?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: It is He Who created you from a single being, and then from the same being created its spouse, and sent down for you eight pairs of animals; He creates you in your mothers’ wombs, from one sort to another, in a triple darkness; such is Allah, your Lord – for Him only is the kingship; there is no God except Him; so where are you being turned away?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He created you of a single soul, then from it He appointed its mate; and He sent down to you of the cattle eight couples. He creates you in your mothers' wombs creation after creation in threefold shadows. That then is God, your Lord; to Him belongs the Kingdom; there is no god but He; so how are you turned about?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: He created you Of a Single soul, and made his spouse therefrom; and of the cattle He sent down Unto you eight pairs. He createth you in the bellies of your mothers, one creation after creation, in a threefold darkness. Such is Allah, your Lord. His is the dominion, there is no god but He. Whither then turn ye away?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He created you (all) from a single person (Adam); then made from him his wife [Hawwa' (Eve)]. And He has sent down for you of cattle eight pairs (of the sheep, two, male and female; of the goats, two, male and female; of the oxen, two, male and female; and of the camels, two, male and female). He creates you in the wombs of your mothers, creation after creation in three veils of darkness, such is Allah your Lord. His is the kingdom, La ilaha illa Huwa (none has the right to be worshipped but He). How then are you turned away?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He created you from one person, then made from it its mate, and brought down livestock for you—eight kinds in pairs. He creates you in the wombs of your mothers, in successive formations, in a triple darkness. Such is God, your Lord. His is the kingdom. There is no god but He. So what made you deviate?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: He it is Who created you from a single being, and He it is Who made from it its mate. He it is Who created for you eight heads of cattle in pairs. He creates you in your mothers' wombs, giving you one form after another in threefold depths of darkness. That, then, is Allah, your Lord. His is the kingdom. There is no god but He. So, whence are you being turned astray?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He created you (all) from a single person; then made from him his wife. And He has sent down for you of An`am eight pairs. He creates you in the wombs of your mothers, creation after creation in three veils of darkness. Such is Allah your Lord. His is the kingdom. La ilaha illa Huwa. How then are you turned away

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: He created you from one being, then from that (being) He made its mate; and He hath provided for you of cattle eight kinds. He created you in the wombs of your mothers, creation after creation, in a threefold gloom. Such is Allah, your Lord. His is the Sovereignty. There is no Allah save Him. How then are ye turned away?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: He created you from a single soul, then made from it its mate, and He has sent down for you eight mates of the cattle. He creates you in the wombs of your mothers, creation after creation, in a threefold darkness. That is Allah, your Lord! To Him belongs all sovereignty. There is no god except Him. Then where are you being led away?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: He created you from a single soul, then from it He created its spouse. And He sent down to you eight pairs of the cattle. He creates you in your mothers' womb, creation after creation, in three (stages of) darkness. Such then is Allah, your Lord. For Him is the Kingdom. There is no god except Him. How, then, can you turn away?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He created you from one soul. Then He made from it its mate, and He produced for you from the grazing livestock eight mates. He creates you in the wombs of your mothers, creation after creation, within three darknesses. That is Allah, your Lord; to Him belongs dominion. There is no deity except Him, so how are you averted?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He has created you from a single soul. Out of this He created your spouse. He sent you eight pairs of cattle. He creates you for a second time in the wombs of your mothers behind three curtains of darkness. He is your Lord to whom belongs the Kingdom. He is the only Lord. Where then will you turn away?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: He has created you from a single being, then made its mate of the same (kind), and He has made for you eight of the cattle in pairs. He creates you in the wombs of your mothers-- a creation after a creation-- in triple darkness; that is Allah your Lord, His is the kingdom; there is no god but He; whence are you then turned away?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He created you from a single soul, then produced its spouse from it, and He has provided for you eight heads of cattle in pairs. He creates you stage by stage in your mothers' wombs in a threefold darkness. Such is God, your Lord. Sovereignty is His. There is no god but Him. So what has made you turn away?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He created you (all) from a single person: then created, of like nature, his mate; and he sent down for you eight head of cattle in pairs: He makes you, in the wombs of your mothers, in stages, one after another, in three veils of darkness. such is Allah, your Lord and Cherisher: to Him belongs (all) dominion. There is no god but He: then how are ye turned away (from your true Centre)?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Khalaqakum min nafsin wahidatin thumma jaAAala minha zawjaha waanzala lakum mina alanAAami thamaniyata azwajin yakhluqukum fee butooni ommahatikum khalqan min baAAdi khalqin fee thulumatin thalathin thalikumu Allahu rabbukum lahu almulku la ilaha illa huwa faanna tusrafoona

7.arabic: إِن تَكْفُرُوا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَنِىٌّ عَنكُمْ ۖ وَلَا يَرْضَىٰ لِعِبَادِهِ ٱلْكُفْرَ ۖ وَإِن تَشْكُرُوا۟ يَرْضَهُ لَكُمْ ۗ وَلَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَىٰ ۗ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُم مَّرْجِعُكُمْ فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If you are ungrateful, then remember! God is independent of you, and He does not favour ingratitude on the part of His creatures. If you are grateful He will be pleased with you. For no one who carries a burden bears another's load; and your returning is to your Lord, when He will tell you what you used to do. Surely He knows what is in the hearts.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: If you become ungrateful, then (know that) indeed Allah is Independent of you; and He does not like the ungratefulness of His bondmen; and if you give thanks, He is pleased with it for you; and no burdened soul will bear another soul’s burden; you have then to return towards your Lord – He will therefore inform you of what you used to do; undoubtedly, He knows what lies within the hearts.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: If you are unthankful, God is independent of you, yet He approves not unthankfulness in His servants; but if you are thankful, He will approve it in you. And no soul laden bears the load of another. Then to your Lord shall you return, and He will tell you what you have been doing. He knows the thoughts within the breasts.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: If ye disbelieve, then verily Allah is Independent of you. And He approveth not of infidelity in His bondmen. And if ye return thanks He approveth of that in you. No burdened soul shall bear another's burthen. Thereafter Unto your Lord is Your return; and He shall declare Unto you that which ye have been Working. Verily He is the Knower of that which is in the breasts.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: If you disbelieve, then verily, Allah is not in need of you, He likes not disbelief for His slaves. And if you are grateful (by being believers), He is pleased therewith for you. No bearer of burdens shall bear the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return, so He will inform you what you used to do. Verily, He is the All-Knower of that which is in (men's) breasts.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: If you disbelieve, God is Independent of you, yet He does not approve ingratitude on the part of His servants. And if you are thankful, He will approve that in you. No bearer of burden can bear the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return; and He will inform you of what you used to do. He is aware of what the hearts contain.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: If you disbelieve, know well that Allah has no need of you. Yet He does not like unbelief in His servants. But if you are thankful, your thankfulness will please Him. No one shall bear another's burden. You are destined to return to your Lord and He will tell you what you used to do. He is well aware even of what lies hidden in your breasts.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: If you disbelieve, then verily, Allah is not in need of you; He likes not disbelief for His servants. And if you are grateful, He is pleased therewith for you. No bearer of burdens shall bear the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return, and He will inform you what you used to do. Verily, He is the All-Knower of that which is in breasts.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: If ye are thankless, yet Allah is Independent of you, though He is not pleased with thanklessness for His bondmen; and if ye are thankful He is pleased therewith for you. No laden soul will bear another's load. Then unto your Lord is your return; and He will tell you what ye used to do. Lo! He knoweth what is in the breasts (of men).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: If you are ungrateful, Allah has indeed no need of you, though He does not approve ingratitude for His servants; and if you give thanks, He approves that for you. No bearer shall bear another’s burden; then your return will be to your Lord, whereat He will inform you concerning what you used to do. Indeed, He knows best what is in the breasts.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: If you disbelieve, Allah is Rich, independent of you. Yet He does not approve of disbelief to His worshipers, but if you believe He will approve it in you. No laden soul shall bear another's load. Then, to Allah you shall return and He will tell to you what you have done. He knows the innermost of your chests.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: If you disbelieve - indeed, Allah is Free from need of you. And He does not approve for His servants disbelief. And if you are grateful, He approves it for you; and no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another. Then to your Lord is your return, and He will inform you about what you used to do. Indeed, He is Knowing of that within the breasts.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: If you disbelieve, know that God is certainly independent of you. He does not want disbelief for His servants. If you give thanks, He will accept it from you. No one will be responsible for the sins of others. To your Lord you will all return and He will tell you about what you have done. He knows best what the hearts contain.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: If you are ungrateful, then surely Allah is Self-sufficient above all need of you; and He does not like ungratefulness in His servants; and if you are grateful, He likes it in you; and no bearer of burden shall bear the burden of another; then to your Lord is your return, then will He inform you of what you did; surely He is Cognizant of what is in the breasts.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: If you are ungrateful, remember that God has no need of you. He is not pleased by ingratitude in His servants; if you are grateful, He is pleased [to see] it in you.  No soul shall bear another's burden. You will return to your Lord in the end and He will declare to you what you have done: He knows well what is in the hearts of men.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: If ye reject (Allah), Truly Allah hath no need of you; but He liketh not ingratitude from His servants: if ye are grateful, He is pleased with you. No bearer of burdens can bear the burden of another. In the end, to your Lord is your Return, when He will tell you the truth of all that ye did (in this life). for He knoweth well all that is in (men's) hearts.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: In takfuroo fainna Allaha ghaniyyun AAankum wala yarda liAAibadihi alkufra wain tashkuroo yardahu lakum wala taziru waziratun wizra okhra thumma ila rabbikum marjiAAukum fayunabbiokum bima kuntum taAAmaloona innahu AAaleemun bithati alssudoori

8.arabic: وَإِذَا مَسَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ ضُرٌّ دَعَا رَبَّهُۥ مُنِيبًا إِلَيْهِ ثُمَّ إِذَا خَوَّلَهُۥ نِعْمَةً مِّنْهُ نَسِىَ مَا كَانَ يَدْعُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ مِن قَبْلُ وَجَعَلَ لِلَّهِ أَندَادًا لِّيُضِلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ ۚ قُلْ تَمَتَّعْ بِكُفْرِكَ قَلِيلًا ۖ إِنَّكَ مِنْ أَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلنَّارِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When man is afflicted with adversity he turns to his Lord, and prays to Him. But when He bestows His favour on him, he forgets what he prayed for before, and sets up others as compeers of God to mislead (people) from His way. Say: "Take advantage of your denying for a while: You will be among the inmates of Hell."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And when some hardship strikes man, he prays to his Lord, inclined only towards Him – then when Allah grants him a favour from Himself, he forgets why he had prayed to Him earlier, and sets up equals to Allah in order to mislead from Allah’s way; proclaim, “Revel in your disbelief for some days; you are indeed of the people of the fire.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: When some affliction visits a man, he calls upon his Lord, turning to him; then when He confers on him a blessing from Him he forgets that he was calling to before and sets up compeers to God, to lead astray from His way. Say: 'Enjoy thy unbelief a little; thou shalt be among the inhabitants of the Fire.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And when some hurt toucheth man, he calleth upon his Lord, turning Unto Him in penitence; then when He bestoweth upon him a favour from Himself, he forgetteth that for which he called on Him afore, and setteth up peers Unto Allah that He may lead astray others from His way Say thou: enjoy thou life in thy infidelity for a while, verily thou art of the fellows of the Fire.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And when some hurt touches man, he cries to his Lord (Allah Alone), turning to Him in repentance, but when He bestows a favour upon him from Himself, he forgets that for which he cried for before, and he sets up rivals to Allah, in order to mislead others from His Path. Say: "Take pleasure in your disbelief for a while: surely, you are (one) of the dwellers of the Fire!"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: When some adversity touches the human being, he prays to his Lord, repenting to Him. But then, when He confers on him a grace of His, he forgets what he was praying for before, and he attributes rivals to God, in order to lead astray from His way. Say, “Enjoy your disbelief for a little while; you will be among the inmates of the Fire.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: When any affliction befalls man, he cries out to his Lord, penitently turning to Him. But when his Lord bestows His favour upon him, he forgets the affliction regarding which he had cried out and sets up compeers to Allah that they may lead others astray from His Path. Say, (O Prophet): “Enjoy your unbelief for a while. Surely you will be among the inmates of the Fire.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And when some hurt touches man, he cries to his Lord, turning to Him in repentance. But when He bestows a favor upon him from Himself, he forgets that for which he cried for before, and he sets up rivals to Allah, in order to mislead others from His path. Say: "Take pleasure in your disbelief for a while, surely, you are (one) of the dwellers of the Fire!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when some hurt toucheth man, he crieth unto his Lord, turning unto Him (repentant). Then, when He granteth him a boon from Him he forgetteth that for which he cried unto Him before, and setteth up rivals to Allah that he may beguile (men) from his way. Say (O Muhammad, unto such an one): Take pleasure in thy disbelief a while. Lo! thou art of the owners of the Fire.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When distress befalls man, he supplicates his Lord, turning to Him penitently. Then, when He grants him a blessing from Himself, he forgets that for which he had supplicated Him earlier and sets up equals to Allah, that he may lead [people] astray from His way. Say, ‘Revel in your ingratitude for a while. Indeed you are among the inmates of the Fire.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When an affliction befalls a human, he supplicates to his Lord and turns to Him (in repentance) yet, no sooner does He bestow on him His blessing than he forgets that for which he had just supplicated and sets up associates with Allah in order to lead (people) away from His Path. Say: 'Enjoy your disbelief for awhile, you shall surely be among the companions of the Fire.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And when adversity touches man, he calls upon his Lord, turning to Him [alone]; then when He bestows on him a favor from Himself, he forgets Him whom he called upon before, and he attributes to Allah equals to mislead [people] from His way. Say, "Enjoy your disbelief for a little; indeed, you are of the companions of the Fire."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When the human being is afflicted with hardship, he starts to pray to his Lord and turns to Him in repentance. When God grants him a favor, he forgets the hardship about which he had prayed to God and starts to consider equal to God things that lead him astray from His path. (Muhammad), tell him, "You can only enjoy in your disbelief for a short time. You will certainly be a dweller of hell fire".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when distress afflicts a man he calls upon his Lord turning to Him frequently; then when He makes him possess a favor from Him, he forgets that for which he called upon Him before, and sets up rivals to Allah that he may cause (men) to stray off from His path. Say: Enjoy yourself in your ungratefulness a little, surely you are of the inmates of the fire.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When man suffers some affliction, he prays to his Lord and turns to Him in penitence, but once he has been granted a favour from God, he forgets the One he had been praying to and sets up rivals to God, to make others stray from His path. Say, "Enjoy your unbelief for a little while: you will be one of the inmates of the Fire."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: When some trouble toucheth man, he crieth unto his Lord, turning to Him in repentance: but when He bestoweth a favour upon him as from Himself, (man) doth forget what he cried and prayed for before, and he doth set up rivals unto Allah, thus misleading others from Allah's Path. Say, "Enjoy thy blasphemy for a little while: verily thou art (one) of the Companions of the Fire!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waitha massa alinsana durrun daAAa rabbahu muneeban ilayhi thumma itha khawwalahu niAAmatan minhu nasiya ma kana yadAAoo ilayhi min qablu wajaAAala lillahi andadan liyudilla AAan sabeelihi qul tamattaAA bikufrika qaleelan innaka min ashabi alnnari

9.arabic: أَمَّنْ هُوَ قَـٰنِتٌ ءَانَآءَ ٱلَّيْلِ سَاجِدًا وَقَآئِمًا يَحْذَرُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ وَيَرْجُوا۟ رَحْمَةَ رَبِّهِۦ ۗ قُلْ هَلْ يَسْتَوِى ٱلَّذِينَ يَعْلَمُونَ وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ ۗ إِنَّمَا يَتَذَكَّرُ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Can one who prays in the watches of the night, bowing in homage or standing attentive, fearful of the life to come, and hoping for the mercy of his Lord, (be like one who does not)? Say: "Can those who know, and those who do not know, be equal? Only they think who are wise."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Will he, whose night hours pass in obedience while prostrating and standing, fearing the Hereafter and hoping for the mercy of his Lord, ever be equal to the disobedient? Proclaim, “Are the knowledgeable and the ignorant equal?” It is only the men of intellect who heed advice.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or is he who is obedient in the watches of the night, bowing himself and standing, he being afraid of the world to come and hoping for the mercy of his Lord..? Say: 'Are they equal -- those who know and those who know not?' Only men possessed of minds remember.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Is he who is devout, in the watches of the night prostrating himself and standing, bewaring of the Hereafter and hoping for the mercy of his Lord to be dealt with like a wicked infidel? Say thou: shall they who know and those who know not be held equal? It is only men of understanding who receive admonition.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Is one who is obedient to Allah, prostrating himself or standing (in prayer) during the hours of the night, fearing the Hereafter and hoping for the Mercy of his Lord (like one who disbelieves)? Say: "Are those who know equal to those who know not?" It is only men of understanding who will remember (i.e. get a lesson from Allah's Signs and Verses).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Is he who worships devoutly during the watches of the night, prostrating himself and standing up, mindful of the Hereafter, and placing his hope in the mercy of his Lord? Say, “Are those who know and those who do not know equal?” Only those possessed of reason will remember.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Is such a person (preferable or he) who is obedient, and prostrates himself in the watches of the night, stands (in Prayer), is fearful of the Hereafter, and looks forward to the mercy of His Lord? Ask them: “Are those who know equal to those who do not know?” Only those endowed with understanding take heed.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Is one who is obedient to Allah, Ana'a Al-Layl prostrating and standing fearing the Hereafter and hoping for the mercy of his Lord Say: "Are those who know equal to those who know not" It is only men of understanding who will remember.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Is he who payeth adoration in the watches of the night, prostrate and standing, bewaring of the Hereafter and hoping for the mercy of his Lord, (to be accounted equal with a disbeliever)? Say (unto them, O Muhammad): Are those who know equal with those who know not? But only men of understanding will pay heed.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Is he who supplicates in the watches of the night, prostrating and standing, apprehensive of the Hereafter and expecting the mercy of his Lord...? Say, ‘Are those who know equal to those who do not know?’ Only those who possess intellect take admonition.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or, is he who is obedient that prostrates himself and stands during parts of the night, being afraid of the Everlasting Life but hoping for the Mercy of the Lord (to be compared to the unbeliever)' Say: 'Are they equal, those who know and those who do not know' Only those with minds remember.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Is one who is devoutly obedient during periods of the night, prostrating and standing [in prayer], fearing the Hereafter and hoping for the mercy of his Lord, [like one who does not]? Say, "Are those who know equal to those who do not know?" Only they will remember [who are] people of understanding.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Can this one be considered equal to one who worships God during the night, prostrating and standing, who has fear of the Day of Judgment, and who has hope in the mercy of his Lord? Say, "Are those who know equal to those who do not know? Only the people of reason take heed".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: What! he who is obedient during hours of the night, prostrating himself and standing, takes care of the hereafter and hopes for the mercy of his Lord! Say: Are those who know and those who do not know alike? Only the men of understanding are mindful.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Is he who prays devoutly to God in the hours of the night, prostrating himself and standing in prayer, who is ever mindful of the life to come and hopes for the mercy of his Lord [like one who does not]? Say, "Are those who know equal to those who do not know?" Truly, only those endowed with understanding will take heed.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Is one who worships devoutly during the hour of the night prostrating himself or standing (in adoration), who takes heed of the Hereafter, and who places his hope in the Mercy of his Lord - (like one who does not)? Say: "Are those equal, those who know and those who do not know? It is those who are endued with understanding that receive admonition.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Amman huwa qanitun anaa allayli sajidan waqaiman yahtharu alakhirata wayarjoo rahmata rabbihi qul hal yastawee allatheena yaAAlamoona waallatheena la yaAAlamoona innama yatathakkaru oloo alalbabi

10.arabic: قُلْ يَـٰعِبَادِ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱتَّقُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ ۚ لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا۟ فِى هَـٰذِهِ ٱلدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةٌ ۗ وَأَرْضُ ٱللَّهِ وَٰسِعَةٌ ۗ إِنَّمَا يُوَفَّى ٱلصَّـٰبِرُونَ أَجْرَهُم بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Tell them: "O my creatures who have come to belief, have fear of displeasing your Lord. There is good for those who do good in this world, and productive is God's earth. Only those who persevere will get their reward measureless."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “O my slaves who have accepted faith! Fear your Lord; for those who do good deeds in this world is goodness (in return); and Allah’s earth is spacious; it is the steadfast who will be paid their full reward, without account.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say: 'My servants who believe, fear your Lord. For those who do good in this world good, and God's earth is wide. Surely the patient will be paid their wages in full without reckoning.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou: O Mine bondmen who believe! fear your Lord. For those who do good in this world there is good: and Allah's earth is spacious. Verily the patient shall be paid in full their hire without reckoning.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "O My slaves who believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism), be afraid of your Lord (Allah) and keep your duty to Him. Good is (the reward) for those who do good in this world, and Allah's earth is spacious (so if you cannot worship Allah at a place, then go to another)! Only those who are patient shall receive their rewards in full, without reckoning."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say, “O My devotees who have believed, keep your duty to your Lord. For those who do good in this world, is goodness. And God’s earth is vast. The steadfast will be paid their wages in full, without reckoning.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Tell them (O Prophet): “O you servants of Mine who believe, have fear of your Lord. A good end awaits those who did good in this world. Allah's earth is spacious. Verily those who persevere shall be granted their reward beyond all reckoning.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Say: "O My servants who believe, have Taqwa of your Lord. Good is for those who do good in this world, and Allah's earth is spacious! Only those who are patient shall receive their reward in full, without reckoning."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Say: O My bondmen who believe! Observe your duty to your Lord. For those who do good in this world there is good, and Allah's earth is spacious. Verily the steadfast will be paid their wages without stint.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘[Allah declares:] “O My servants who have faith! Be wary of your Lord. For those who do good in this world there will be a good [reward], and Allah’s earth is vast. Indeed the patient will be paid in full their reward without any reckoning.” ’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say: 'My worshipers who believe, fear your Lord. For those who do good in this world there is good and the earth of Allah is wide surely, those who are patient will be recompensed in full without count'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, "O My servants who have believed, fear your Lord. For those who do good in this world is good, and the earth of Allah is spacious. Indeed, the patient will be given their reward without account."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Say to My believing servants, "Have fear of your Lord. Those who act righteously in this life will receive good reward. The land of God is vast. God will recompense the deeds of those who have exercised patience, without keeping an account".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say: O my servants who believe! be careful of (your duty to) your Lord; for those who do good in this world is good, and Allah's earth is spacious; only the patient will be paid back their reward in full without measure.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say, "[God says] O My servants who have believed, fear your Lord. For those who do good in this world will have a good reward -- and God's earth is spacious. Truly, those who persevere patiently will be requited without measure."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say: "O ye my servants who believe! Fear your Lord, good is (the reward) for those who do good in this world. Spacious is Allah's earth! those who patiently persevere will truly receive a reward without measure!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul ya AAibadi allatheena amanoo ittaqoo rabbakum lillatheena ahsanoo fee hathihi alddunya hasanatun waardu Allahi wasiAAatun innama yuwaffa alssabiroona ajrahum bighayri hisabin

11.arabic: قُلْ إِنِّىٓ أُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَعْبُدَ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِصًا لَّهُ ٱلدِّينَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Say: "I am commanded to worship God with obedience all-exclusive for Him;

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Say, “I am commanded that I must worship Allah as His sincere bondman.” (And so must you too.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say: 'I have been commanded to serve God making my religion His sincerely;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou: verily I am commanded to worship Allah, making for Him religion exclusive.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Verily, I am commanded to worship Allah (Alone) by obeying Him and doing religious deeds sincerely for Allah's sake only and not to show off, and not to set up rivals with Him in worship;

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say, “I was commanded to serve God, devoting my religion exclusively to Him.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Tell them, (O Prophet): “I am bidden to serve Allah, consecrating my devotion to Him,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Say: "Verily, I am commanded to worship Allah, making religion sincerely for Him."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Say (O Muhammad): Lo! I am commanded to worship Allah, making religion pure for Him (only).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘I have been commanded to worship Allah with exclusive faith in Him,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say: 'I am ordered to worship Allah making my religion sincerely His.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, [O Muhammad], "Indeed, I have been commanded to worship Allah, [being] sincere to Him in religion.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Say, "I am commanded to worship God and be devoted to His religion

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say: I am commanded that I should serve Allah, being sincere to Him in obedience.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say, "I have been commanded to serve God, dedicating my worship entirely to Him.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say: "Verily, I am commanded to serve Allah with sincere devotion;

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul innee omirtu an aAAbuda Allaha mukhlisan lahu alddeena

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And I am commanded to be the first of those who submit."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And I am commanded that I be the first to submit.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and I have been commanded to be the first of those that surrender.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And I am commanded this, in order I may be the first Of those who submit.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And I am commanded (this) in order that I may be the first of those who submit themselves to Allah (in Islam) as Muslims."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And I was commanded to be the first of those who submit.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and I am bidden to be the first of those who surrender to Him.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "And I am commanded (this) in order that I may be the first of the Muslims."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And I am commanded to be the first of those who are muslims (surrender unto Him).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and I have been commanded to be the foremost of those who submit [to Him].’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: I am ordered to be the first of those to be submissive (Muslims to Him)'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And I have been commanded to be the first [among you] of the Muslims."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: and I am commanded to be the first Muslim".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And I am commanded that I shall be the first of those who submit.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: I have been commanded to be the first to submit."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "And I am commanded to be the first of those who bow to Allah in Islam."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waomirtu lian akoona awwala almuslimeena

13.arabic: قُلْ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ إِنْ عَصَيْتُ رَبِّى عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Say: "If I disobey my Lord, I fear the punishment of an evil Day."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Say, “Were I to disobey my Lord, I too would fear the punishment of the great Day (of Resurrection).”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say: 'Truly I fear, if I should rebel against my Lord, the chastisement of a dreadful day.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou: verily fear, if I disobeyed my Lord, torment of a Day mighty.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Verily, if I disobey my Lord, I am afraid of the torment of a great Day."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say, “I fear, if I disobeyed my Lord, the punishment of a horrendous Day.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Say: “If I disobey my Lord, I fear the chastisement of an Awesome Day.”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Say: "Verily, if I disobey my Lord, I am afraid of the torment of a great Day."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Say: Lo! if I should disobey my Lord, I fear the doom of a tremendous Day.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘Indeed, should I disobey my Lord, I fear the punishment of a tremendous day.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say: 'Indeed, if I rebel against my Lord I fear the punishment of a dreadful Day'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, "Indeed I fear, if I should disobey my Lord, the punishment of a tremendous Day."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Say, "I am afraid that for disobeying my Lord I shall suffer the torment of the great day".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say: I fear, if I disobey my Lord, the chastisement of a grievous day.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say, "I fear, if I disobey my Lord, the punishment of a Terrible Day."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say: "I would, if I disobeyed my Lord, indeed have fear of the Penalty of a Mighty Day."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qul innee akhafu in AAasaytu rabbee AAathaba yawmin AAatheemin

14.arabic: قُلِ ٱللَّهَ أَعْبُدُ مُخْلِصًا لَّهُۥ دِينِى

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Say: "I worship God with devotion all-exclusive for Him.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Proclaim, “I worship only Allah, as His sincere bondman.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Say: 'God I serve, making my religion His sincerely;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Say thou: it is Allah I worship, making for Him my religion exclusive

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW) "Allah Alone I worship by doing religious deeds sincerely for His sake only and not to show-off, and not to set up rivals with Him in worship."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Say, “It is God I worship, sincere in my faith in Him.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Say: “Allah alone shall I serve, consecrating my devotion to Him.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Say: "Allah Alone I worship by doing religious deeds sincerely for His sake only."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Say: Allah I worship, making my religion pure for Him (only).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Say, ‘I worship [only] Allah, putting my exclusive faith in Him.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Say: 'I worship Allah and make my religion sincerely His.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Say, "Allah [alone] do I worship, sincere to Him in my religion,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Say, "I worship God alone and devote myself to His religion.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Say: Allah (it is Whom) I serve, being sincere to Him in my obedience:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Say, "It is God I serve, sincere in my faith in Him alone --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Say: "It is Allah I serve, with my sincere (and exclusive) devotion:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Quli Allaha aAAbudu mukhlisan lahu deenee

15.arabic: فَٱعْبُدُوا۟ مَا شِئْتُم مِّن دُونِهِۦ ۗ قُلْ إِنَّ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ ٱلَّذِينَ خَسِرُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَأَهْلِيهِمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۗ أَلَا ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْخُسْرَانُ ٱلْمُبِينُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: You may worship what you will apart from Him." Say: "Surely the greatest losers will be those who will lose their own selves and their people on the Day of Resurrection." Remember, this will be an all-too-evident loss.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “Therefore you can worship what you want besides Him!” Say, “The worst losers are those who will lose themselves and their household on the Day of Resurrection; pay heed! This is the plain loss!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: so serve what you will apart from Him.' Say: 'Surely the losers are they who lose themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection; is not that the manifest loss?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: So worship whatsoever ye will, besidev him. Say thou: verily the losers are those who shall have lost themselves and their household" on the Day of judgment LO! that will be a loss manifest.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So worship what you like besides Him. Say (O Muhammad SAW): "The losers are those who will lose themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection. Verily, that will be a manifest loss!"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “But you can worship whatever you wish besides Him.” Say, “The losers are those who lose their souls and their people on the Day of Resurrection.” That is indeed the obvious loss.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So serve, apart from Him, whomsoever you please.” Say: “Behold, the real losers shall be those who will have lost their own selves and their kith and kin on the Day of Resurrection. Behold, that is the obvious loss.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "So, worship what you like besides Him." Say: "The losers are those who will lose themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection. Verily, that will be a manifest loss!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then worship what ye will beside Him. Say: The losers will be those who lose themselves and their housefolk on the Day of Resurrection. Ah, that will be the manifest loss!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: You worship whatever you wish besides Him.’ Say, ‘The losers are those who ruin themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection.’ Indeed, that is a manifest loss!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (As for yourselves) worship what you will, other than Him' Say: 'The losers are surely those who lose themselves and their families on the Day ofResurrection. Is that not a clear loss'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So worship what you will besides Him." Say, "Indeed, the losers are the ones who will lose themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection. Unquestionably, that is the manifest loss."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Worship besides Him whatever you want. The greatest losers are those whose souls and family members will be lost on the Day of Judgment for this is certainly a great loss".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Serve then what you like besides Him. Say: The losers surely are those who shall have lost themselves and their families on the day of resurrection; now surely that is the clear loss.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: as for yourselves, worship anything you please besides Him!" Say, "The real losers will be those who lose themselves and all their kith and kin on the Day of Resurrection. That is the [most] obvious loss.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Serve ye what ye will besides him." Say: "Truly, those in loss are those who lose their own souls and their People on the Day of Judgment: Ah! that is indeed the (real and) evident Loss!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: FaoAAbudoo ma shitum min doonihi qul inna alkhasireena allatheena khasiroo anfusahum waahleehim yawma alqiyamati ala thalika huwa alkhusranu almubeenu

16.arabic: لَهُم مِّن فَوْقِهِمْ ظُلَلٌ مِّنَ ٱلنَّارِ وَمِن تَحْتِهِمْ ظُلَلٌ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ يُخَوِّفُ ٱللَّهُ بِهِۦ عِبَادَهُۥ ۚ يَـٰعِبَادِ فَٱتَّقُونِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Above them will be a covering of fire, below them a cloud (of flames). With this does God warn His creatures: "O My creatures, fear Me."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Above them are mountains of fire and beneath them are mountains of fire; Allah scares His bondmen with this; “O My bondmen! Fear Me!”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Above them they shall have overshadowings. of the Fire, and underneath them overshadowings; that it is wherewith God frightens His servants: "O My servants, so fear you Me!"'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: For them! above them shall be coverings of Fire and beneath them coverings. Therewith Allah affrighteth His bondmen. O My bondmen! wherefore fear Me.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They shall have coverings of Fire, above them and covering (of Fire) beneath them; with this Allah does frighten His slaves: "O My slaves, therefore fear Me!"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They will have layers of Fire above them, and layers beneath them. That is how God strikes fear into His servants—“O My servants! Beware of Me!”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: There shall be sheets of fire above them and beneath them. This is the end against which Allah warns His servants. So dread My wrath, O you servants of Mine!”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They shall have coverings of Fire, above them and covering (of Fire) beneath them. With this Allah frightens His servants: "O My servants, therefore have Taqwa of Me!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They have an awning of fire above them and beneath them a dais (of fire). With this doth Allah appal His bondmen. O My bondmen, therefor fear Me!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: There will be canopies of fire above them, and [similar] canopies beneath them. With that Allah deters His servants. So, My servants, be wary of Me!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Above them they shall have layers of fire and beneath them layers. By this Allah puts fear into His worshipers' 'O My worshipers, fear Me'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They will have canopies of fire above them and below them, canopies. By that Allah threatens His servants. O My servants, then fear Me.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Above and below them their will be shadows of fire. This is how God frightens His servants. My servants have fear of Me.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They shall have coverings of fire above them and coverings beneath them; with that Allah makes His servants to fear, so be careful of (your duty to) Me, O My servants!

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They will have sheets of fire above them and sheets of fire beneath them." That is how God puts fear into the hearts of His servants. Fear me, then, My servants.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They shall have Layers of Fire above them, and Layers (of Fire) below them: with this doth Allah warn off his servants: "O My Servants! then fear ye Me!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Lahum min fawqihim thulalun mina alnnari wamin tahtihim thulalun thalika yukhawwifu Allahu bihi AAibadahu ya AAibadi faittaqooni

17.arabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ ٱجْتَنَبُوا۟ ٱلطَّـٰغُوتَ أَن يَعْبُدُوهَا وَأَنَابُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ لَهُمُ ٱلْبُشْرَىٰ ۚ فَبَشِّرْ عِبَادِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: There are happy tidings for those who keep away from the worship of false gods and turn to God in repentance. Give glad tidings to My creatures.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And those who stayed away from the worship of idols, and inclined towards Allah – for them are glad tidings; therefore give glad tidings to My bondmen. –

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Those who eschew the serving of idols and turn penitent to God, for them is good tidings! So give thou good tidings to My servants

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And those who avoid the devils lest they should worship them and turn Unto Allah in penitence, for them are glad tidings. Wherefore give thou glad tidings Unto My bondmen,

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Those who avoid At-Taghut (false deities) by not worshipping them and turn to Allah in repentance, for them are glad tidings; so announce the good news to My slaves,

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: As for those who avoid the worship of idols, and devote themselves to God—theirs is the good news. So give good news to My servants.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (On the other hand), good tidings await those who eschew serving false gods and penitently return to Allah. (O Prophet), give good tidings to My servants,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Those who avoid At-Taghut by not worshipping them and turn to Allah, for them are glad tidings; so announce the good news to My servants.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And those who put away false gods lest they should worship them and turn to Allah in repentance, for them there are glad tidings. Therefor give good tidings (O Muhammad) to My bondmen

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: As for those who stay clear of the worship of fake deities and turn penitently to Allah, there is good news for them. So give good news to My servants

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those who shun the worship of idols and turn in repentance to Allah for them glad tidings. Therefore give good tidings to My worshipers,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: But those who have avoided Taghut, lest they worship it, and turned back to Allah - for them are good tidings. So give good tidings to My servants

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Those who have avoided worshipping idols and have turned in repentance to God will receive the glad news.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And (as for) those who keep off from the worship of the idols and turn to Allah, they shall have good news, therefore give good news to My servants,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: There is good news for those who shun the worship of false deities and turn to God, so give good news to My servants,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Those who eschew Evil,- and fall not into its worship,- and turn to Allah (in repentance),- for them is Good News: so announce the Good News to My Servants,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waallatheena ijtanaboo alttaghoota an yaAAbudooha waanaboo ila Allahi lahumu albushra fabashshir AAibadi

18.arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يَسْتَمِعُونَ ٱلْقَوْلَ فَيَتَّبِعُونَ أَحْسَنَهُۥٓ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ هَدَىٰهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمْ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Those who listen to the Word and then follow the best it contains, are the ones who have been guided by God, and are men of wisdom.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Those who heed attentively and follow the best from it; it is these whom Allah has guided, and it is these who have intelligence.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: who give ear to the Word and follow the fairest of it. Those are they whom God has guided; those -- they are men possessed of minds.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Who hearken Unto the word and follow that which is the exceLlent there of. These are they whom Allah hath guided, and those are men of understanding.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Those who listen to the Word [good advice La ilaha ill-Allah (none has the right to be worshipped but Allah) and Islamic Monotheism, etc.] and follow the best thereof (i.e. worship Allah Alone, repent to Him and avoid Taghut, etc.) those are (the ones) whom Allah has guided and those are men of understanding (like Zaid bin 'Amr bin Nufail, Salman Al-Farisi and Abu Dhar Al-Ghifari). [Tafsir Al-Qurtubi, Vol. 12, P. 244]

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who listen to the Word, and follow the best of it. These are they whom God has guided. These are they who possess intellect.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: to those who pay heed to what is said and follow the best of it. They are the ones whom Allah has guided to the Right Way; they are the ones endowed with understanding.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Those who listen to the Word and follow the best thereof, those are (the ones) whom Allah has guided and those are men of understanding.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Who hear advice and follow the best thereof. Such are those whom Allah guideth, and such are men of understanding.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: who listen to the word [of Allah] and follow the best [interpretation] of it. They are the ones whom Allah has guided, and it is they who possess intellect.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: who listen to the Words and follow what is finest of it. These are they whom Allah has guided. They are those of understanding.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Who listen to speech and follow the best of it. Those are the ones Allah has guided, and those are people of understanding.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), give the glad news to those of Our servants who listen to the words and only follow the best ones. Tell them that they are those whom God has guided. They are the people of understanding.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Those who listen to the word, then follow the best of it; those are they whom Allah has guided, and those it is who are the men of understanding.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: who listen to what is said and follow what is best in it. These are the ones God has guided; these are the people endowed with understanding.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Those who listen to the Word, and follow the best (meaning) in it: those are the ones whom Allah has guided, and those are the ones endued with understanding.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Allatheena yastamiAAoona alqawla fayattabiAAoona ahsanahu olaika allatheena hadahumu Allahu waolaika hum oloo alalbabi

19.arabic: أَفَمَنْ حَقَّ عَلَيْهِ كَلِمَةُ ٱلْعَذَابِ أَفَأَنتَ تُنقِذُ مَن فِى ٱلنَّارِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Can he against whom the sentence of punishment has been justified (be rescued)? Can you save him who is in the Fire?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So will the one upon whom the Word of punishment has proved true, ever be equal to those who are forgiven? So will you guide and save the one who deserves the fire? (Because the hearts of some disbelievers are sealed).

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: He against whom the word of chastisement is realized -- shalt thou deliver him out of the Fire?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Is he then on whom is justified the decree of torment - wilt thou rescue him who is in the Fire?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Is, then one against whom the Word of punishment justified (equal to the one who avoids evil). Will you (O Muhammad SAW) rescue him who is in the Fire?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: What about someone who has deserved the sentence of punishment? Is it you who can save those in the Fire?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (O Prophet), can you save him (from chastisement) against whom the sentence of chastisement has become due; him who has, (as it were), already fallen into the Fire?”

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Is, then one against whom the Word of punishment is justified (equal to the one who avoids evil) Will you rescue him who is in the Fire

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Is he on whom the word of doom is fulfilled (to be helped), and canst thou (O Muhammad) rescue him who is in the Fire?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Can he against whom the word of punishment has become due…? Can you rescue someone who is in the Fire?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Can you save from the Fire he whom against the word of punishment has been realized?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then, is one who has deserved the decree of punishment [to be guided]? Then, can you save one who is in the Fire?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: How can you rescue the one who is destined to suffer the torment?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: What! as for him then against whom the sentence of chastisement is due: What! can you save him who is in the fire?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But what of him against whom the sentence of punishment is justified? Can you rescue one who is already in the Fire?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
